turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Plate -->
  <circle cx="256" cy="290" r="150" fill="#f3f6ff" stroke="#1f2a44" stroke-width="6"/>
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="325" rx="115" ry="45" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.8"/>

  <!-- Food (steak) -->
  <path d="M205 285 C205 250 230 225 256 225 C282 225 307 250 307 285 C307 323 280 350 256 350 C232 350 205 323 205 285 Z" fill="#c86b3a" stroke="#6b2e18" stroke-width="4"/>
  <path d="M225 270 C240 255 255 250 270 256 C255 272 245 288 235 305 C230 295 220 282 225 270 Z" fill="#e79a6a" opacity="0.9"/>

  <!-- Fork -->
  <g transform="translate(310,145) rotate(12)">
    <rect x="0" y="90" width="18" height="160" rx="8" fill="#d9dde8" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="4" y="70" width="10" height="30" rx="5" fill="#d9dde8"/>
    <g>
      <rect x="0" y="250" width="18" height="22" rx="6" fill="#d9dde8" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4"/>
      <path d="M3 205 L3 235" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M9 205 L9 235" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M15 205 L15 235" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M3 205 L9 205" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.0"/>
      <path d="M3 210 L8 210" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.0"/>
    </g>
    <g>
      <path d="M4 235 L8 262" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M10 235 L14 262" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M16 235 L20 262" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Prongs -->
    <g transform="translate(0,170)">
      <path d="M4 0 L4 40" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M14 0 L14 40" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
      <path d="M9 0 L9 40" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- Knife -->
  <g transform="translate(160,170) rotate(-12)">
    <rect x="0" y="60" width="110" height="22" rx="10" fill="#d9dde8" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4"/>
    <rect x="85" y="78" width="55" height="16" rx="8" fill="#d9dde8" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.95"/>
    <rect x="90" y="95" width="25" height="150" rx="10" fill="#d9dde8" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4"/>
    <path d="M95 240 L135 190" stroke="#29324a" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Table shading -->
  <path d="M70 430 C145 380 215 375 256 385 C307 397 378 402 442 430 L442 512 L70 512 Z" fill="#e9edf7" opacity="0.9"/>
  <path d="M100 445 C165 410 220 405 258 413 C310 425 360 430 410 445" fill="none" stroke="#c7d0ea" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating visual representation**

I'm thinking about how to draw the concept of "eat" without using any text or letters. I want to represent it with simple shapes, like a plate, fork, and knife, and maybe include a food item like a carrot or a piece of steak. This could work as a one-word guessing game, likely leading to "eat." I need to make sure it's simple, so SVG is the way to go!

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

**Identifying the icon**

I’m trying to figure out what this icon represents. It might look like a "drum" or a "barrel" due to its orange-brown base. The gray handle could resemble a grill, or possibly it’s part of a "pump." Then there’s a dark blue outline that makes me think of "fire," but it could also symbolize a "planet" with a telescope. After some reflection, my best guess is it's a "telescope." I'll generate 20 unique, one-word guesses related to astronomy focused on that theme.I’m working on creating a list of one-word terms related to astronomy. So far, I’ve got: telescope, observatory, astronomy, stargazer, stargazing (which I’ll count), space, universe, planet, earth, solar, cosmos, satellite, spacecraft, comet, orbit, galaxy, stars, sky, surveyor, lens, and binoculars. I’ll double-check for duplicates and keep everything to one word each. I think "stargazing" is acceptable, and "solar" can work too. Finally, I’ll format this as JSON.
